mission.title: open-webui: feat: knowledge/projects (revamped documents section)
mission.description: feat: knowledge/projects (revamped documents section). Requested by 26+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
#3521 #3231 #2044 #3110 #3464 #1096
 - Upload file via Files API
 - Process and link file to project(s) using file id
 - Chat with Files using project id(s) in chat completion payload

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: knowledge/projects (revamped documents section)
steps.description:
This pull request implements bulk tagging and untagging functionality for selected documents, addressing the feature request in issue #3110. This enhancement improves workflow efficiency by allowing users to manage tags for multiple documents simultaneously.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: knowledge/projects (revamped documents section)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This pull request implements bulk tagging and untagging functionality for selected documents, addressing the feature request in issue #3110. This enhancement improves workflow efficiency by allowing users to manage tags for multiple documents simultaneously.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
